chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْبَدَنَةِ وَ الْبَقَرَةِ عَنْ كَمْ تُجْزِئُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 184 – The (sacrificial) camel and the cow, on behalf of how many (people) it would suffice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/176)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/176)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مُتَمَتِّعٌ لَمْ يَجِدْ هَدْياً فَقَالَ أَ مَا كَانَ مَعَهُ دِرْهَمٌ يَأْتِي بِهِ قَوْمَهُ فَيَقُولَ أَشْرِكُونِي بِهَذَا الدِّرْهَمِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Qar’at, from Zayd Bin Jaham who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘The performer of Tumatto could not find a sacrificial animal’. So heasws said: ‘Did he not have one Dirham to go to his people with, so he could be saying to them, ‘Participate me with this Dirham?’
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hafs ibn Qar‘ah from Zayd ibn Jahm who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is for al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous) and cannot find an offering. He (the Imam) said, ‘Does he not have any dirham to ask his people to allow him to share with them?”’ (Fatwa best explains Hadith 2-5 of this Chapter)
